title: memory_save tags are stored as comma-joined TEXT — cannot query, comma in tag corrupts round-trip
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness (1) / Architectural Alignment (2)

Location: src/memory.rs:356-364 memory_save; 390-407 memory_search split(',') parse; schema at line 231 'tags TEXT DEFAULT'.

Violation: memory_save takes tags: &[String] and stores them as tags.join(",") in a single TEXT column. Every read path splits on ','. Problems:

1. A tag containing a literal comma (e.g. tag='rust, tokio') is split into two fake tags on read. No escaping. Data corruption on round-trip.
2. Cannot query 'all memories tagged X' efficiently — no index, only substring LIKE which yields false matches (e.g. LIKE '%rust%' matches 'rustaceans').
3. First normal form violation. Tags are a many-to-many — should be a junction table archival_memory_tags(memory_id, tag).
4. FTS5 is indexing the tag blob as one giant term. Searching for a tag via FTS uses substring semantics instead of exact match.
5. Empty tag list = empty string — split(',') returns [''], which is then filtered at line 398 with 'filter(|s| !s.is_empty())'. OK but wasteful allocation.

Mandated Refactor:
1. Schema v4: CREATE TABLE archival_memory_tags (memory_id INTEGER REFERENCES archival_memory(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, tag TEXT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY(memory_id, tag)); CREATE INDEX ON archival_memory_tags(tag).
2. memory_save wraps the INSERT + N tag-INSERTs in a single transaction.
3. memory_search JOINs to filter by tag explicitly via a parameter.
4. Backfill migration: parse the old tags column, write into the new table, drop the old column.
5. Keep the FTS index on archival_memory.content only; tags filter is a separate JOIN.
